detaileddescription.para: Stitch to (x, y).
Move to (x, y).
Trim and move to (x, y).
Pause machine for a thread change.
Add a sequin at the current co-ordinates.
End of program.
Type of sector Compound File Sector (CFS)
Type of directory object Probably unallocated
a directory type object
a file type object
the root entry
Special values for Stream Identifiers All real stream Ids are less than this
There is no valid stream Id
Should some platform need a different precision, this is typedef-ed and used in place of all real types where possible.
EmbColor uses the light primaries: red, green, blue in that order.
The basic type to represent points absolutely or represent directions.
Positive y is up, units are in mm.
For our internal string library.
Note that we cannot use this for any larger amount of data, it's to ensure that the cap on the size is fixed at 200.
To help new developers understand why we use "void *", when it is widely not recommended within C++.
libembroidery is a low-level library: we need to do bit-level and untyped calculations. Thinking "memory location" not "untyped pointer" helped me (Robin).
The basic array type.

xrefdescription.para: color should be called colorIndex since it is not an EmbColor.

detaileddescription.para: absolute position (not relative)
Ellipse object.
The pattern type variable denotes the type that was read in and uses the EMB_FORMAT contants. Changing this type directly would break how data is interpreted,
Thread colors that are subject to change are loaded at runtime, allowing us to update them as they change.
However, in-builts that use indicies like the DXF, SVG or HUS tables are compiled in. This should help with embedded compatibility in both cases.
This uses about 100kb per instance because it's not dynamic.
double-indirection file allocation table references

memberdef.argsstring: (L_system L, char *state, int iteration, int complete)
memberdef.name: lindenmayer_system

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned char r, unsigned char g, unsigned char b)
memberdef.name: embColor_make

memberdef.argsstring: (EmbReal x1, EmbReal y1, EmbReal x2, EmbReal y2)
memberdef.name: emb_line_make

briefdescription.para: Finds the unit length vector a result in the same direction as a vector.
The scalar multiple a magnitude of a vector. Returned as a vector.
The sum of two vectors returned as a vector.
The average of two vectors returned as a vector.
The difference between two vectors returned as a result.
The dot product of two vectors returned as a EmbReal.
The "cross product" as vectors a and b returned as a real value.

detaileddescription.para: Technically, this is the magnitude of the cross product when the embroidery is placed in the z=0 plane (since the cross product is defined for 3-dimensional vectors).

Since we aren't using full 3D vector algebra here, all vectors are "vertical". so this is like the product v1^{T} I_{2} v2 for our vectors a v1 and v2 so a "component-wise product". The result is stored at the pointer a result.

briefdescription.para: The length or absolute value of the vector a vector.
The unit vector in the direction a angle.

memberdef.argsstring: (EmbReal cx, EmbReal cy, EmbReal rx, EmbReal ry)
memberdef.name: embEllipse_make

memberdef.argsstring: (EmbPattern *p, EmbReal x, EmbReal y, int flags, int isAutoColorIndex)
memberdef.name: emb_pattern_addStitchAbs

memberdef.argsstring: (EmbPattern *p, EmbReal dx, EmbReal dy, int flags, int isAutoColorIndex)
memberdef.name: emb_pattern_addStitchRel

detaileddescription.para: Adds a point object to pattern (a p) at the absolute position (a x,a y). Positive y is up. Units are in millimeters.

memberdef.argsstring: (EmbReal bulge, EmbArc arc, EmbVector center, EmbReal radius, EmbReal diameter, EmbReal chord, EmbVector chordMid, EmbReal sagitta, EmbReal apothem, EmbReal incAngle, char clockwise)
memberdef.name: printArcResults

detaileddescription.para: Compress data "data" of length "length" to "output" with length "output_length". Returns whether it was successful as an int.
This avoids the now unnecessary compression by placing a minimal header of 6 bytes and using only literals in the huffman compressed part (see the sources above).

memberdef.argsstring: (FILE *file, int x, int y, int flags)
memberdef.name: pfaffEncode

memberdef.argsstring: (FILE *file, unsigned int fatSectors, const unsigned int sectorSize)
memberdef.name: bcf_difat_create

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned char a1, unsigned char a2, unsigned char a3)
memberdef.name: pfaffDecode

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned char b[3], int x, int y, int flags)
memberdef.name: encode_t01_record

memberdef.argsstring: (unsigned char b[3], int x, int y)
memberdef.name: encode_tajima_ternary

detaileddescription.para: LIBEMBROIDERY HEADER version 1.0.0-alpha This file is part of libembroidery.
A library for reading, writing, altering and otherwise processing machine embroidery files and designs.
Also, the core library supporting the Embroidermodder Project's family of machine embroidery interfaces.
Copyright 2018-2025 The Embroidermodder Team Licensed under the terms of the zlib license.
Note that all new defines start with "EMB_", all functions with "emb" and all typedefs with "Emb" to protect against namespace pollution. (INCOMPLETE)
